title: aglaia maingayi
intro: Aglaia maingayi, also known as the yellow aglaia, is a plant species in the family Meliaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Aglaia maingayi include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, and it may also be used in traditional remedies such as poultices and ointments.
